chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي أُصُولِ الْكُفْرِ وَ أَرْكَانِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 115 – The origins of the disbelief and its corner-stones

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (10/85)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (10/85)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَ لَا أُخْبِرُكُمْ بِشِرَارِ رِجَالِكُمْ قُلْنَا بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ إِنَّ مِنْ شِرَارِ رِجَالِكُمُ الْبَهَّاتَ الْجَرِي‏ءَ الْفَحَّاشَ الْآكِلَ وَحْدَهُ وَ الْمَانِعَ رِفْدَهُ وَ الضَّارِبَ عَبْدَهُ وَ الْمُلْجِئَ عِيَالَهُ إِلَى غَيْرِهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, altogether from Ibn Mahboub, from Ibn Raib, from Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Jabir Bin Abdullah who said, ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Shall I<sup>saww</sup> inform you all with the most evil of your men?’ We said, ‘Yes, O Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>! So he<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘From the most evil of your men is the slanderer, the audaciously immoral, the lone eater, and the preventer of the gifts, and the whipper of his slave, and the causer of his family taking shelter with others’.
en.sarwar: 13. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father all from ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab from abu Hamza from Jabir ibn ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah (a.s.) once asked, ‘Should I tell you who is the most wicked among your men?’ They said, ‘Yes, O Messenger of Allah.’ He said, ‘Of the wicked ones among you is a man, accusing, boldly foulmouthed, eating all alone, refusing to give any gifts, beats up his slave and causes his family to seek refuge in others.’”
